GLCT.module.title: GLUniverse — Clocks & Tracker
GLCT.moduleConfig.title: Module Configuration
GLCT.moduleConfig.label: Configure Modules
GLCT.moduleConfig.hint: Enable or disable each module and sub-module. Turn off whatever your game doesn't need — the whole time tracker, the resource tracker, or individual pieces of either. Disabling a section also disables everything nested under it.
GLCT.features.timeHud.name: Time Tracker (HUD)
GLCT.features.timeHud.hint: The top-bar clock & calendar HUD. Disable to remove the time tracker entirely.
GLCT.features.timeHud.calendar.name: Calendar & date
GLCT.features.timeHud.calendar.hint: Click the date to open the calendar view, plus the Alt+C shortcut.
GLCT.features.timeHud.events.name: Events & holidays
GLCT.features.timeHud.events.hint: The upcoming-event badge on the HUD and event entries in the calendar.
GLCT.features.timeHud.mission.name: Mission countdown
GLCT.features.timeHud.mission.hint: The deadline countdown meter and its 🎯 Mission dock button.
GLCT.features.timeHud.shiftMode.name: Watch / stretch view toggle
GLCT.features.timeHud.shiftMode.hint: The dock button that switches between the watch dial and the detailed clock. When off, the HUD stays in stretch view.
GLCT.features.timeHud.sceneTint.name: Scene tint glow
GLCT.features.timeHud.sceneTint.hint: Tint the canvas to match the current watch (opt-in per scene).
GLCT.features.timeHud.gmControls.name: GM time controls
GLCT.features.timeHud.gmControls.hint: The advance / next-shift / set-time buttons on the GM dock.
GLCT.features.trackers.name: Resource Trackers
GLCT.features.trackers.hint: Live, at-a-glance trackers (points, clocks, pools, tasks, hazards).
GLCT.features.trackers.dock.name: Tracker dock
GLCT.features.trackers.dock.hint: The floating tracker dock HUD and its toggle/shortcut.
GLCT.features.trackers.sheet.name: Private trackers on PC sheets (PF2e)
GLCT.features.trackers.sheet.hint: Adds a Trackers tab to each PF2e character sheet for trackers private to that PC.
GLCT.features.weather.name: Weather (Hex Flower)
GLCT.features.weather.hint: The opt-in Hex Flower weather engine wired into the calendar.
GLCT.features.weather.hudChip.name: Weather chip on the HUD
GLCT.features.weather.hudChip.hint: The animated weather readout on the time bar. When off, weather still runs and the flower window still opens.
GLCT.features.support.name: Mission Support
GLCT.features.support.hint: The Support NPC roster and Comms-Coin HUD.
GLCT.features.support.passiveTokenIcon.h: Show the active support's passive Effect icon on each party member's token.
GLCT.features.delving.name: Delving Mode
GLCT.features.delving.hint: The turn-driven dungeon-crawl mode with degrading delving resources.
GLCT.hud.dragHint: Drag to reposition · double-click to switch standard / compact
GLCT.hud.gmTimeHint: GM only — exact time & stretch progress within this watch
GLCT.controls.rewindHint: Left-click to advance · Right-click to rewind
GLCT.controls.missionToggle: Set a mission — count down the stretches to a target time
GLCT.controls.glitchToggle: Temporal distortion — corrupt the date & time into noise for a scene where the when & where is a mystery (affects every player)
GLCT.mission.title: Mission Countdown
GLCT.mission.hint: Pin a target and the stretch meter counts down to it: every stretch between now and the target stays individually visible so players can see exactly how many are left.
GLCT.settings.calendar.name: Active Calendar Definition
GLCT.settings.calendar.hint: The stored custom calendar used by this world.
GLCT.settings.events.name: Calendar Events & Holidays
GLCT.settings.events.hint: Stored events and holidays for the active calendar.
GLCT.settings.hudPosition.name: HUD Position
GLCT.settings.hudPosition.hint: Saved screen position of the HUD.
GLCT.settings.hudCollapsed.name: HUD Collapsed
GLCT.settings.sceneLight.name: Tint Scene With Shift
GLCT.settings.sceneLight.hint: Opt-in per scene: tint the canvas to match the current watch.
GLCT.settings.shiftMode.name: Track Time at Shift Level
GLCT.settings.shiftMode.hint: Focus the HUD on the current watch and hide the clock & stretch meter. Time still advances in stretches; this only changes the display. The GM can also flip this from the HUD dock.
GLCT.settings.stepMode.name: Default Step Snapping
GLCT.settings.stepMode.hint: When advancing, snap to the nearest stretch or shift boundary.
GLCT.editor.title: Calendar Editor
GLCT.calendarEditor.hint: Edit the calendar definition below (Foundry native CalendarConfig). Use Load to start from a preset, or Import a JSON file. Save applies it to this world.
GLCT.calendarEditor.imported: Calendar JSON loaded into the editor. Review, then Save.
GLCT.calendarEditor.resetConfirm: Discard the custom calendar and revert to the selected preset?
GLCT.shiftNames.title: Watch Names
GLCT.shiftNames.label: Edit Watch Names
GLCT.shiftNames.hint: Rename the four daily watches (shifts) shown in the HUD, in chronological order from midnight. Leave a field blank to use its default name.
GLCT.events.title: Events & Holidays
GLCT.events.name: Name
GLCT.events.notePrivateHint: Only the GM can see this
GLCT.events.emptyHint: No events yet. Add holidays, festivals, or one-off events.
GLCT.calendarView.title: Calendar
GLCT.tracker.title: Trackers
GLCT.tracker.pickType: What kind of tracker would you like to add?
GLCT.tracker.emptyHint: No trackers yet — press + to add one.
GLCT.tracker.noGM: No GM is connected to roll this pool.
GLCT.tracker.field.name: Name
GLCT.tracker.field.subtitle: Subtitle
GLCT.tracker.field.label: Label (optional)
GLCT.tracker.settings.sheetTab.name: Private trackers on PC sheets (PF2e)
GLCT.tracker.settings.sheetTab.hint: Adds a “Trackers” tab to each PF2e character sheet for trackers private to that PC — visible and editable only by the actor's owner(s) and the GM, separate from the shared tracker dock.
GLCT.weather.chipHint: Weather — click to open the Hex Flower
GLCT.weather.forecastHint: Chance of each condition on the next step, from the current hex under the Navigation Hex dice.
GLCT.weather.rollHint: Left-click to advance one step · Right-click to rewind
GLCT.weather.resetHint: Reset the walk to the start hex
GLCT.weather.resetConfirm: Reset the weather walk to its start hex? History is cleared.
GLCT.weather.notConfigured: No weather flower is configured yet.
GLCT.weather.field.name: Label
GLCT.weather.settings.enabled.name: Enable Weather
GLCT.weather.settings.enabled.hint: Opt in to the Hex Flower weather system. Ships off so calendar-only worlds are unaffected. A weather chip appears on the HUD once enabled and a flower is configured.
GLCT.weather.settings.cadenceMode.name: Weather Cadence
GLCT.weather.settings.cadenceMode.hint: Auto: the weather walks forward as in-game time passes. Manual: only the GM's Roll-now control advances it.
GLCT.weather.settings.cadencePeriod.name: Weather Step Period
GLCT.weather.settings.cadencePeriod.hint: How much in-game time passes per auto weather step.
GLCT.weather.settings.playerFlower.name: Reveal Flower to Players
GLCT.weather.settings.playerFlower.hint: When on, players can see the full Hex Flower (read-only). Otherwise they see only the current condition and recent history.
GLCT.weather.settings.showDice.name: Roll 3D Dice for Weather
GLCT.weather.settings.showDice.hint: When on, the Navigation Hex roll is thrown as real 3D dice (requires the Dice So Nice! module) on manual steps and single-period auto advances. Multi-day skips stay silent to avoid an animation flood.
GLCT.weather.settings.cardVisibility.hin: Who sees the weather-change announcement in chat. Public: posted to everyone. GM only: whispered to the GMs, so players discover the weather in-fiction. The current condition stays readable on the HUD either way.
GLCT.weather.editor.title: Weather Editor
GLCT.weather.editor.menuHint: Design the Hex Flower: per-season weather, the Navigation Hex, effects, presets, and JSON import/export.
GLCT.weather.editor.clickHex: Click a hex to edit it.
GLCT.weather.editor.imported: Weather loaded into the editor. Review, then Save.
GLCT.weather.editor.invalidIndex: Season “{season}” has invalid or duplicate hex indices.
GLCT.weather.editor.invalidDir: Season “{season}” has an invalid direction in its Navigation Hex.
GLCT.weather.editor.invalidDisallow: Season “{season}” has an invalid blocked direction on a hex.
GLCT.weather.editor.seasonalHint: When on, this region uses a different flower + Navigation Hex per calendar season. When off, one flower is used all year.
GLCT.weather.editor.seasonalOnHint: Seasonal: the calendar season picks the flower (edit each via the tabs below).
GLCT.weather.editor.seasonalOffHint: Single flower for all time — the calendar season is ignored; only the first flower is used.
GLCT.weather.editor.disallowHint: Checked directions are blocked from this hex: rolling one keeps the walk here. Each blocked face is marked with a red ✕ on the flower (the cookbook's red Ø).
GLCT.weather.region.label: Active region
GLCT.weather.region.switchHint: Switch which region drives the HUD and chat. Every region keeps its own weather.
GLCT.weather.region.deleteConfirm: Delete the region “{name}” and its weather walk? This can't be undone.
GLCT.support.expandHint: Click to open · drag to move
GLCT.support.musterHint: Pick the support NPC active for this mission
GLCT.support.pickAndStart: Start a new mission (refill the pool)
GLCT.support.noRoster: No supports in the roster yet. Open the editor to author one?
GLCT.support.noDetail: No details authored for this ability.
GLCT.support.startMissionHint: Refill the pool (Downed-last-mission starts at half) and reset radio use
GLCT.support.recoverHint: Recover: restore to a full pool and clear Downed (Infirmary)
GLCT.support.togglePlayersHint: Show / hide the support coin for players
GLCT.support.clearHint: Clear the active support (removes the passive aura)
GLCT.support.cantInvoke: You can't trigger this support's actions.
GLCT.support.isDowned: This support is Downed and can't act until the next mission.
GLCT.support.radioUsed: This support has already acted this round.
GLCT.support.clearRoundHint: Clear this round's action (let this support act again)
GLCT.support.noGM: An active GM is required to resolve a support action.
GLCT.support.settings.enabled.name: Enable Mission Support
GLCT.support.settings.enabled.hint: Turn on the Support NPC roster + Comms-Coin HUD. PF2e worlds get computed action cards and auto-applied passives; other systems get the coin, pool, and text cards.
GLCT.support.settings.passiveTokenIcon.h: When on, the active support's passive Effect shows its icon on each party member's token. Turn off to keep the buff applied but hide the icon from tokens (it still appears on the character sheet / effects panel).
GLCT.support.editor.title: Mission Support — Roster
GLCT.support.editor.menuHint: Author the support NPC roster: levels, pools, factions, portraits, and the four ability slots.
GLCT.support.editor.empty: No supports yet — add one or import the preset.
GLCT.support.editor.selectHint: Select a support to edit, or add one.
GLCT.support.editor.name: Name
GLCT.support.editor.downFloor: Downed → unavailable until Recover (instead of half pool)
GLCT.support.editor.framePortraitHint: Position the portrait per surface (coin / card / expanded)
GLCT.support.editor.frameNoImg: Set a portrait image first, then frame it.
GLCT.support.editor.frameDragHint: Drag inside a preview to position.
GLCT.support.editor.passiveEffect: Passive effect (drag a PF2e Effect here)
GLCT.support.editor.dropEffect: drag an Effect item, or paste its UUID
GLCT.support.editor.effectUuids: Linked effects (one UUID per line; @effect[0] references the first)
GLCT.support.editor.dropEffects: drag Effect items here, or paste UUIDs
GLCT.support.editor.deleteConfirm: Delete the support “{name}”? This can't be undone.
GLCT.support.editor.presetImported: Imported Razor & Tourniquet ({linked} passives created & linked) — set portraits if you like, then Save.
GLCT.support.editor.importIntro: Paste a support bundle — a single NPC { support, passiveEffect } or a roster { roster, effects } — or load a .json file. The passive PF2e Effect item is created and linked automatically.
GLCT.support.editor.importBadShape: Unrecognised bundle — expected a "support" object or a "roster" array.
GLCT.support.editor.importNoSupport: No supports found in the bundle.
GLCT.support.editor.importSkillHint: Tip: the "support-npc-author" Claude skill writes this JSON for you — describe the NPC, then paste the result here.
GLCT.support.editor.importDone: Imported {n} support(s) — {linked} linked, {fx} effect(s) created. Review, then Save.
GLCT.delving.settings.enabled.name: Enable Delving Mode
GLCT.delving.settings.enabled.hint: Turn on Delving Mode — a turn-driven mode of play. Time still advances (effects expire, weather walks), but the clock readout steps aside for a turn counter and degrading delving-resource displays (torches, corruption…). The GM flips it on/off from the HUD dock at any time.
GLCT.delving.featuredHint: Featured delving resource — drives the HUD atmosphere. Left-click the dice to add one, right-click to remove one; right-click the block for more.
GLCT.delving.stageDown: Step back to the previous stage
GLCT.delving.confirmReset: Start a new delve? This zeroes the turn counter and refills every resource to its first stage.
GLCT.delving.controls.passHint: Pass one turn — advance time, roll every resource, and (every N turns) the weather. Right-click to rewind a turn.
GLCT.delving.controls.rollHint: Roll the featured resource's pool now, without passing a turn — an ad-hoc check. Right-click a resource on the HUD for its own roll.
GLCT.delving.controls.toggleHint: Switch Delving Mode on/off (time keeps advancing; only the display changes)
GLCT.delving.card.endedNotice: This pool is depleted — its delve has already ended.
GLCT.delving.editor.title: Delving Editor
GLCT.delving.editor.menuHint: Configure what a turn represents, how often the weather rolls, and the delving resources (staged dice pools) with their atmospheres.
GLCT.delving.editor.endNamePlaceholder: When fully depleted (blank = final stage name)
GLCT.delving.editor.weatherEveryHint: 0 = never auto-roll weather on a turn. While delving is live the normal time-based weather cadence is suspended.
GLCT.delving.editor.turnHint: One “{label}” advances {count} {unit} and rolls every resource.
GLCT.delving.editor.empty: No resources yet — add one, or load a preset.
GLCT.delving.editor.needResource: Add at least one resource before saving.
GLCT.delving.editor.imported: Delving configuration imported — review, then Save.
